title: Implementation Survey: Bounded ATMS, Baselines, OpenRouter Models, CLUTRR Splits
summary: Comprehensive implementation-ready survey covering: (1) Python ATMS repos and bounded label-propagation pseudocode; (2) LINC/Logic-LM/PoT/ProbLog baseline repo interfaces; (3) OpenRouter model IDs and pricing; (4) CLUTRR per-hop statistics and stratified subsampling plan.

best_repo.name: Ruggiero-Santo/NATMS
best_repo.description: Python ATMS with negation extension (NATMS). Extends de Kleer's general labeling algorithm. More complete than FellnerDotDev variant (also incomplete). Contains ATMS.py with UPDATE, WEAVE, PROPAGATE, NOGOOD functions. Labels are set[frozenset].
best_repo.completeness: Implements core algorithm. Missing: depth cap, explicit size bound on label growth.

secondary_repo.name: FellnerDotDev/ATMS-in-Python
secondary_repo.description: Python 3, de Kleer reference. Described as 'not complete' in README. File: atms_algo.py. 2 commits, 1 star. Good orientation but not production-ready.

data_structures.Assumption: String or symbol identifier marking a node as assumable. Each assumption node has itself as its sole environment: label = {frozenset({a})}.
data_structures.Environment: frozenset of Assumption names. Represents a minimal consistent set of assumptions supporting a proposition.
data_structures.Label: set[frozenset] — the set of minimal environments supporting a node. Minimality invariant: no env in label is a superset of another.
data_structures.NogoodDB: list[frozenset] — list of minimal inconsistent assumption sets. When an env is a superset of any no-good, it is removed from all labels.
data_structures.Node: Object with fields: proposition (str), label (set[frozenset]), justifications (list of Justification). A Justification = (list[Node] antecedents, Node consequent).

missing_features_to_add: depth_cap: filter out any environment E from a label where len(E) > d (depth parameter). Implement as: env_set = {e for e in env_set if len(e) <= d}
size_cap: optionally truncate total number of environments per label to avoid exponential blowup. Implement as: label = set(sorted(label, key=len)[:max_envs])
No-good pruning efficiency: NATMS iterates all nodes on NOGOOD — add indexed lookup by assumption for speed.
Incremental justification addition: existing code handles this but may need explicit test.

bounded_atms_algorithm.depth_definition: The 'depth' of an environment E is len(E) — the number of assumptions in E. A depth cap d=3 means no environment with >3 assumptions is tracked. This approximates bounded prime-implicate computation (de Kleer 1992 AAAI).
bounded_atms_algorithm.no_good_handling: NOGOOD(E): for all nodes n, n.label = {e for e in n.label if not E <= e}. Propagate changes. Store E in atms.nogoods.
references: de Kleer, J. (1986). An assumption-based TMS. Artificial Intelligence, 28(2), 127-162.
de Kleer, J. (1992). An improved incremental algorithm for generating prime implicates. AAAI-92, pp. 780-785.

LINC.paper: LINC: A Neurosymbolic Approach for Logical Reasoning (EMNLP 2023, arXiv:2310.15164)
LINC.llm_api: OpenAI API (GPT-3.5/GPT-4). Redirect to OpenRouter by patching the OpenAI base_url to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 and setting OPENAI_API_KEY to OpenRouter key.
LINC.custom_dataset_approach: LINC takes premises/conclusions in natural language; parse via args.py to find the dataset loading code; custom data should be formatted as the same schema as FOLIO/ProofWriter (JSON with premises list + conclusion + label). Exact format needs args.py inspection.
LINC.k_sample_voting: K-sample parameter likely in args.py. Not documented in README; inspect runner.py for --n_samples or similar flag.
LINC.clutrr_integration: Kinship premises (e.g. 'Alice is Bob's mother') map to FOL directly. Each k-hop story becomes a set of FOL premises + query. Feasible to adapt.
LINC.confidence: Medium — entry point and prover confirmed; exact custom-dataset arg format needs local code inspection.

Logic_LM.solver: SMT solver (code modified from SatLM https://github.com/xiye17/sat-lm). Likely Z3-backed.
Logic_LM.llm_backend_swap: Modify OpenAIModel class in utils.py. Pass OpenRouter base_url and key. --model_name supports any string that the API accepts.
Logic_LM.custom_dataset_format: Add prompt template at ./models/prompts/{dataset_name}.txt with [[PROBLEM]] and [[QUESTION]] placeholders. Add dataset loader to match existing dataset structure in ./data/
Logic_LM.confidence: High — entry points, solver, and LLM config confirmed from source inspection.

PoT_Path_of_Thoughts.note: No public GitHub repository found for arXiv:2412.17963 (Path-of-Thoughts, Zhang et al. 2024/2026). Authors did not release code as of June 2026.

stage_1_graph_extraction.description: Single LLM call: given a story, extract all (subject, relation, object) triples and identify the query entities. Output: JSON graph G = {nodes: [...], edges: [{src, rel, dst}], query: {src, dst}}.
stage_1_graph_extraction.prompt_strategy: Use sectional markup, syntactic delimiters, predefined relation set (kinship relations), task decomposition. Templates in paper Appendix A.4-A.6.

stage_2_path_identification.description: Deterministic DFS over the extracted graph. Find all simple paths from query src to query dst. No LLM call.
stage_2_path_identification.algorithm: DFS with visited set; return all paths as lists of edge triples.

stage_3_reasoning.description: For each path, make an independent LLM call (PoT-LLM variant) or run ASP/CLINGO (PoT-Symbolic). Return majority-vote or first non-None answer.
stage_3_reasoning.symbolic_option: CLINGO (Answer Set Programming) with kinship rules encoded as ASP facts

reimplementation_spec.clutrr_loading: Use HuggingFace CLUTRR/v1 test set. Paper modifies stories to remove name tagging and embed query as natural language question. 1,049 test samples (k=2-10).
reimplementation_spec.performance_claim: Up to 21.3% improvement over SotA baselines without fine-tuning.

PoT_Path_of_Thoughts.confidence: High for reimplementation spec — paper describes method in detail. Stage 3 requires access to prompts from Appendix A.4-A.6 not fully reproduced online.

ProbLog.key_syntax: Probabilistic fact: '0.8::parent(tom, bob).' — use p::fact. notation. Rules: standard Prolog. Query: query(term).
ProbLog.platform_note: get_evaluatable() auto-selects d-DNNF or SDD based on OS. Works on Linux/Mac/Windows.
ProbLog.confidence: Very high — official docs and PyPI package confirmed.

model_table.json_support: Yes (tool use / structured output via OpenAI-compatible API)
model_table.notes: MoE 284B total / 13B activated. Released 2026-04-24. Best value for L1.
Strong reasoning + tool use. Good for L2 assumption proposal.
Free tier available (rate limited). Good fallback.
Strong reasoning but output is expensive at $2.50/M. Avoid for high-volume.
Free but rate limited. Only for testing.

L1_grounding_json_extraction.rationale: Cheapest reliable model with JSON/structured output. At $0.0983/$0.1966 per M, adequate for span-to-atom extraction.

L2_assumption_proposal.rationale: Stronger reasoning, controllable reasoning behavior. At $0.2288/$0.3432 per M, affordable for ~400 calls.

budget_estimate.scope: 400 documents (200 CLUTRR + 100 bAbI + 100 custom), 2 L1 calls + 1 L2 call per doc
budget_estimate.budget_headroom: $10 budget → ~11.6x headroom. Comfortable for 3 reruns, ablations, and debug iterations.
budget_estimate.note: Even running all 4 baselines with the same compute budget stays well under $10.

clutrr_splits.paper_citation: Sinha et al. (2019). CLUTRR: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Inductive Reasoning from Text. EMNLP 2019. arXiv:1908.06177.

per_hop_counts.source: Reported in graph-based synthetic data augmentation study (arXiv:2409.12437 Table 2); consistent with PoT paper (1,049 test samples k=2-10)

train_set.note: Standard split trains on k=2,3,4 only; tests on k=2-10 for generalization.

configurations.gen_train23_test2to10: Train k={2,3}, test k=2-10. Standard generalization task.
configurations.gen_train234_test2to10: Train k={2,3,4}, test k=2-10. Easier training regime.

huggingface_load.load_call:
from datasets import load_dataset
ds = load_dataset('CLUTRR/v1', 'gen_train23_test2to10', trust_remote_code=True)
test = ds['test']  # 1857 examples, k=2-10
huggingface_load.alternative_data_id: data_089907f8 (pre-generated Google Drive variant used in original paper; train k=2,3, test k=2-10)

stratified_subsample_200.method: Equal allocation across 9 strata (k=2 to k=10), 22 per stratum = 198 total
stratified_subsample_200.note: All strata have at least 114 instances. No stratum exhaustion. Use random.seed(42) for reproducibility.
stratified_subsample_200.code_snippet:
import random
from datasets import load_dataset
from collections import defaultdict

random.seed(42)
ds = load_dataset('CLUTRR/v1', 'gen_train23_test2to10', trust_remote_code=True)['test']

# Group by hop count
buckets = defaultdict(list)
for ex in ds:
    k = ex['num_hops']  # or len(ex['relation_list']) depending on field name
    buckets[k].append(ex)

# Sample 22 per stratum
subsample = []
for k in range(2, 11):
    stratum = buckets.get(k, [])
    n = min(22, len(stratum))
    subsample.extend(random.sample(stratum, n))
print(f'Total subsample: {len(subsample)}')

answer:
## Phase 1: Python ATMS Implementations

Two Python ATMS implementations exist on GitHub. The best is **Ruggiero-Santo/NATMS** [2] — a negation-extended ATMS based on de Kleer's general labeling algorithm. It implements UPDATE, WEAVE, PROPAGATE, and NOGOOD functions. Labels are `set[frozenset]`; environments are `frozenset` of assumption names [2]. The secondary option is **FellnerDotDev/ATMS-in-Python** [1], which its README explicitly describes as incomplete.

Neither repo implements a **depth cap** (filter environments where `len(E) > d`) or a **size cap** on label cardinality — both must be added [2]. De Kleer (1992) [17] described incremental prime-implicate generation for ATMS but no simple bounded variant; the natural approximation is depth(E) = len(E). The bounded label-propagation pseudocode:

```
for each justification J:
  env_set = cross_product(labels of J.antecedents)
  env_set = prune_supersets(env_set)
  env_set = filter_nogoods(env_set, nogood_db)   # remove supersets of any no-good
  env_set = {e for e in env_set if len(e) <= d}  # depth cap
  node.label = merge_minimal(node.label, env_set)
```

Data structures: Environment = frozenset; Label = set[frozenset]; NogoodDB = list[frozenset] [2].

## Phase 2: Baseline Interfaces

**LINC** [3] (EMNLP 2023): Entry point `accelerate launch runner.py`; FOL prover is Prover9 [3]. Config in `linc/eval/args.py`. LLM API redirectable to OpenRouter by setting `OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`. Custom datasets require format matching FOLIO/ProofWriter JSON schema [3].

**Logic-LM** [4] (EMNLP 2023): Two-step pipeline — `models/logic_program.py` (generation) then `models/logic_inference.py` (inference) [4]. Symbolic solver is SMT/Z3-based (code from SatLM). LLM swappable by modifying `OpenAIModel` class in `utils.py`. Custom datasets: add `./models/prompts/{dataset_name}.txt` with `[[PROBLEM]]`/`[[QUESTION]]` placeholders [4].

**Path-of-Thoughts** [5] (arXiv:2412.17963): No public code released as of June 2026 [5]. Must reimplement from paper [6]. Three stages: (1) single LLM call to extract entity-relation triples and build a graph; (2) deterministic DFS to find all source-to-target paths; (3) per-path LLM inference or ASP/CLINGO symbolic reasoning [6]. Paper uses CLUTRR test set modified to 1,049 samples (k=2–10); achieves up to 21.3% improvement over SOTA [6].

**ProbLog** [7] (ML-KULeuven): `pip install problog`. Minimal Python API [8]:
```python
from problog.program import PrologString
from problog import get_evaluatable
model = "0.8::parent(tom,bob). 0.9::parent(bob,ann). ancestor(X,Y):-parent(X,Y). query(ancestor(tom,ann))."
result = get_evaluatable().create_from(PrologString(model)).evaluate()
# => {ancestor(tom,ann): 0.72}
```
Probabilistic facts use `p::fact.` syntax; `query(term).` marks evaluation targets [8].

## Phase 3: OpenRouter Models and Pricing

Confirmed June 2026 pricing [9–12]: `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` [9] costs $0.0983/$0.1966 per million tokens with 1M context. `deepseek/deepseek-v3.2` [10] costs $0.2288/$0.3432 per million tokens with 131K context. `google/gemini-2.5-flash` [11] costs $0.30/$2.50 per million (expensive output). `meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct` [12] costs $0.10/$0.32 per million; free tier available. Free-tier models (google/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it:free etc.) are rate-limited [18].

Recommended pairing: L1 JSON extraction → `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` [9]; L2 assumption proposal → `deepseek/deepseek-v3.2` [10].

Budget estimate: 400 docs × 2 L1 calls = 800 calls at $0.087 total; 400 L2 calls at $0.128 total; ×3 baseline overhead ≈ $0.86 total [9, 10, 12]. With a $10 budget this leaves 11.6× headroom.

## Phase 4: CLUTRR Dataset

Per-hop test instance counts confirmed from two sources [14, 16]: k=2(114), k=3(229), k=4(219), k=5(308), k=6(178), k=7(246), k=8(228), k=9(172), k=10(163) — total 1,857 test instances [16]. Training covers k=2,3 (or k=2,3,4); test always spans k=2–10 for generalization evaluation [14]. The PoT paper uses 1,049 of these test samples [6].

HuggingFace load [15]: `load_dataset('CLUTRR/v1', 'gen_train23_test2to10', trust_remote_code=True)['test']`. Six total configurations available [15].

Stratified 200-instance subsample [16]: 9 strata (k=2–10) × 22 per stratum = 198 instances. All strata are feasible — minimum available is 114 instances at k=2 [16]. Use `random.seed(42)` for reproducibility.
follow_up_questions: PoT reimplementation: The paper's Appendix A.4-A.6 contains the exact LLM prompts for graph extraction and per-path reasoning. These are not reproduced in the arXiv HTML. Can the executor use a generic extraction prompt or should we fetch the ar5iv version with appendix content?

LINC custom dataset format: The exact JSON schema for passing custom CLUTRR instances to LINC requires inspecting linc/eval/args.py locally. What fallback should the executor use if LINC is not easily adaptable to CLUTRR?

OpenRouter gemini-2.5-flash-lite pricing: The costgoat.com table did not show this model separately. Is google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite available on OpenRouter, and at what price, as a potential cheaper L2 option than gemini-2.5-flash?
